title: Combinatorial Algorithm for Optimal Colouring of Perfect Graphs
statement_text: Construct a ``combinatorial'' polynomial-time algorithm that yields an optimal colouring of a perfect graph.
context_text: Grötschel, Lovász, and Schrijver [9] showed in 1988 that the ellipsoid method finds an $\omega(G)$-colouring of any perfect graph in polynomial time. Finding a combinatorial algorithm achieving the same has remained a well-known open question in the field ever since.
paper_contribution: Gives a combinatorial algorithm (Algorithm 1.1) that, with input an $n$-vertex Berge graph, outputs an optimal colouring in time $O(n^{(\omega(G)+1)^2})$, which is polynomial for fixed clique number $k = \omega(G)$.
notes: Described in the paper as a 'well-known open question' with no explicit citation to an originating author; it arises naturally from the Grötschel–Lovász–Schrijver [9] result. PDF source.
paper_title: Colouring perfect graphs with bounded clique number

title: Open Question on Number of Tight Skew Partitions
statement_text: We do not know how many tight skew partitions there can really be; the authors do not know of a graph with more than linearly many tight skew partitions.
context_text: The paper shows there are at most $n^3 \log n$ tight skew partitions in an $n$-vertex graph (improving the $n^4$ bound from the Kennedy–Reed list), because for every tight skew partition $(A,B)$ the set $B$ appears at least $k_2^2$ times in the Kennedy–Reed output, where $k_2$ is the size of the second-largest anticomponent of $G[B]$. The true maximum is unknown.
notes: Stated as prose without a labelled theorem environment. PDF source.
paper_title: Colouring perfect graphs with bounded clique number
